subject: [N5] 34 action items from 2025-10-10 spv-hmya-oeh
body:
Hi V,

I extracted 34 action items from your 2025-10-10 spv-hmya-oeh meeting (20251028-132902_Meetings).
Please review and reply with your approval:

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

**1. # DETAILED_RECAP**
   📅 Wednesday 2:00pm | ⏱ 30m | 🟡 Normal
   📁 Operations | 🏷 meeting
   📝 From meeting recap...

**2. **
   📅 Wednesday 2:00pm | ⏱ 30m | 🟡 Normal
   📁 Operations | 🏷 none
   📝 From meeting recap...

**3. Feedback**: - [ ] Useful**
   📅 Wednesday 2:00pm | ⏱ 30m | 🟡 Normal
   📁 Operations | 🏷 none
   📝 From meeting recap...

**4. **
   📅 Wednesday 2:00pm | ⏱ 30m | 🟡 Normal
   📁 Operations | 🏷 none
   📝 From meeting recap...

**5. ## Key Decisions and Agreements**
   📅 Wednesday 2:00pm | ⏱ 30m | 🟡 Normal
   📁 Operations | 🏷 none
   📝 From meeting recap...

**6. **Exit-focused strategy within one year** - The team has decided to pivot from ongoing operations to actively pursuing acquisition within 12 months. Rationale: Current runway constraints combined with uncertain revenue trajectory make a strategic exit more viable than continued fundraising. This matters because it fundamentally shifts priorities from product development to positioning for acquisition.**
   📅 Wednesday 2:00pm | ⏱ 30m | 🟡 Normal
   📁 Product | 🏷 none
   📝 From meeting recap...

**7. **Team restructuring to lean operations** - Decision to reduce US-based overhead and shift work to India team to extend runway. Specific changes: Rockle not needed immediately, Ilsa potentially part-time. This preserves cash while maintaining essential technical capabilities for sale preparation.**
   📅 Wednesday 2:00pm | ⏱ 30m | 🟡 Normal
   📁 Operations | 🏷 none
   📝 From meeting recap...

**8. **Explore roll-up model positioning** - Investigate framing Careerspan as a recruiting agency roll-up company rather than pure tech play. The idea: Position as acquiring and modernizing traditional recruiting firms, leveraging Careerspan tech to improve recruiter-to-candidate ratios and profitability. This matters because it opens access to different investor pools (PE-backed startups targeting archaic industries) and creates a bigger fundraising narrative.**
   📅 Wednesday 2:00pm | ⏱ 30m | 🟡 Normal
   📁 Personnel | 🏷 none
   📝 From meeting recap...

**9. **$5M acquisition target established** - Agreed on $5M as the minimum viable exit that provides meaningful returns: ~$1.3M for Vrijen, ~$600K for Logan, ~$400K for Ilsa. This concrete target enables working backwards to identify specific acquisition scenarios and prepare appropriate proof points.**
   📅 Wednesday 2:00pm | ⏱ 30m | 🟡 Normal
   📁 Operations | 🏷 none
   📝 From meeting recap...

**10. **War-game acquisition targets next week** - Logan and Vrijen to meet in Boston (Monday or Tuesday) to map acquisition landscape systematically: who has deep pockets, what are they acquiring for (tech vs. distribution), what proof points are needed for each scenario.**
   📅 Wednesday 2:00pm | ⏱ 30m | 🟡 Normal
   📁 Operations | 🏷 none
   📝 From meeting recap...

**11. **Apply to Work Tech Sherm** - Following up on recent call and Laura's recommendation. Deadline approaching, need to coordinate with team on application.**
   📅 Wednesday 2:00pm | ⏱ 30m | 🟡 Normal
   📁 Operations | 🏷 follow_up
   📝 From meeting recap...

**12. ## Strategic Context**
   📅 Wednesday 2:00pm | ⏱ 30m | 🟡 Normal
   📁 Operations | 🏷 none
   📝 From meeting recap...

**13. Market Positioning Tension:** The conversation reveals a core strategic question about whether to sell on technical capabilities (AI verification, background checks, resume-to-interview consistency) or distribution strength (embedded in communities, user volume, unique data access). The tech angle is harder to make objective cases for but potentially higher value; distribution is easier to demonstrate but requires more community partnerships.**
   📅 Wednesday 2:00pm | ⏱ 30m | 🟡 Normal
   📁 Operations | 🏷 none
   📝 From meeting recap...

**14. Runway Reality:** Without additional capital, the team needs to make hard choices. The "leaky bucket" concern (putting money into a business without proven revenue) is driving the exit-focused approach rather than raising more funds. This is pragmatic but also reflects market conditions where career tech consolidation is accelerating.**
   📅 Wednesday 2:00pm | ⏱ 30m | 🟡 Normal
   📁 Operations | 🏷 none
   📝 From meeting recap...

**15. Roll-Up Model Opportunity:** Vrijen's observation about Google Ventures/Compadre Capital showcasing three roll-up companies (targeting accountants, business automation) suggests a viable fundraising path exists for companies acquiring and modernizing legacy industries. The recruiting industry fits this pattern: fragmented firms with proprietary networks and qualitative judgment that could be centralized and tech-enabled.**
   📅 Wednesday 2:00pm | ⏱ 30m | 🟡 Normal
   📁 Personnel | 🏷 none
   📝 From meeting recap...

**16. AI Democratization Paradox:** Interesting meta-observation that LLMs are "pseudo-democratizing" rather than truly democratizing - powerful enough to 4x capability for those who can wield them (like Vrijen with Zo Computer), but still requiring significant effort/sophistication. This creates a temporary advantage window for technical founders who can bridge the gap.**
   📅 Wednesday 2:00pm | ⏱ 30m | 🟡 Normal
   📁 Operations | 🏷 none
   📝 From meeting recap...

**17. Community vs. Enterprise Pathways:** Two viable positioning strategies emerged:**
   📅 Wednesday 2:00pm | ⏱ 30m | 🟡 Normal
   📁 Operations | 🏷 none
   📝 From meeting recap...

**18. 1. Consumer/community play (distribution strength, embedded relationships, user volume)**
   📅 Wednesday 2:00pm | ⏱ 30m | 🟡 Normal
   📁 Operations | 🏷 none
   📝 From meeting recap...

**19. 2. Enterprise tech play (background verification, AI-powered hiring tools, API/SDK integrations)**
   📅 Wednesday 2:00pm | ⏱ 30m | 🟡 Normal
   📁 Operations | 🏷 none
   📝 From meeting recap...

**20. The former plays to Vrijen and Logan's networking strengths; the latter requires more technical validation but opens doors to companies like Checker (which just made a $2B acquisition in verification space).**
   📅 Wednesday 2:00pm | ⏱ 30m | 🟡 Normal
   📁 Networking | 🏷 none
   📝 From meeting recap...

**21. ## Critical Next Action**
   📅 Wednesday 2:00pm | ⏱ 30m | 🟡 Normal
   📁 Operations | 🏷 none
   📝 From meeting recap...

**22. Owner:** Vrijen + Logan**
   📅 Wednesday 2:00pm | ⏱ 30m | 🟡 Normal
   📁 Operations | 🏷 none
   📝 From meeting recap...

**23. Deliverable:** Complete acquisition target ecosystem map with proof points matrix**
   📅 Wednesday 2:00pm | ⏱ 30m | 🟡 Normal
   📁 Operations | 🏷 none
   📝 From meeting recap...

**24. Timeline:** In-person session in Boston, Monday or Tuesday of next week**
   📅 Wednesday 2:00pm | ⏱ 30m | 🟡 Normal
   📁 Operations | 🏷 none
   📝 From meeting recap...

**25. Purpose:** Systematically identify all potential acquirers segmented by what they would buy for (tech capability vs. distribution/data), research their acquisition history, map proof points needed for each scenario, and create outreach strategy leveraging existing networks. This provides the roadmap for next 12 months of positioning work.**
   📅 Wednesday 2:00pm | ⏱ 30m | 🟡 Normal
   📁 Networking | 🏷 none
   📝 From meeting recap...

**Reply with:**
• "approve all" → Push all to Akiflow
• "approve 1,34" → Push only #1 and #34
• "skip 2" → Don't create #2
• "edit 1: tomorrow 8am, 30m" → Modify then push

Or just reply "approved" and I'll push everything!

—Zo
